entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop whose grain yield can be affected by temperature extremes during flowering.

entities.name: EXTREME TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Abnormally high or low temperatures that can impact crop development and yield.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of grain production per unit area of the farm.

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The phenological stage of the wheat crop sensitive to temperature extremes.

entities.name: ZONING
entities.description: The process of dividing a farm area into distinct zones based on temperature risk.

entities.name: SOUTHERN MALLEE, VICTORIA, AUSTRALIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the farm-scale study was conducted.

entities.name: DATA LOGGERS
entities.description: Devices used to spatially record daily temperature courses at a specific height.

entities.name: ZONING ALGORITHMS
entities.description: Computational procedures used to categorize the farm into different temperature zones.

entities.name: SPATIAL INTERPOLATION
entities.description: A technique used to produce maps of temperature zones from recorded data points.

entities.name: ARCVIEW 3.2
entities.description: GIS software used to perform spatial interpolation and generate temperature maps.
